title: Definition of domestic violence
text: Any act, omission or commission that harms or injures or has the potential to harm or injure the health, safety, life, limb, or well-being of the aggrieved person constitutes domestic violence. This includes physical, sexual, verbal, emotional, and economic abuse.

title: Information to Protection Officer and police officer
text: Any person who has reason to believe that an act of domestic violence has been, or is being, committed may give information about it to the Protection Officer or police officer.

title: Duties of police officers, Protection Officers and Magistrate
text: Upon receiving a complaint, the police officer or Protection Officer must inform the aggrieved person about her right to file an application for relief, right to free legal aid, right to file a complaint under Section 498A of IPC, and availability of shelter homes.

title: Duties and functions of Protection Officers
text: The Protection Officer shall assist the Magistrate in discharging functions under this Act, maintain lists of service providers, make domestic incident reports, and ensure the aggrieved person receives required medical aid and legal aid.

title: Service providers
text: Service providers are registered voluntary associations or companies who can provide legal aid, medical facilities, counseling, or shelter to the aggrieved person.

title: Application to Magistrate
text: An aggrieved person or Protection Officer may present an application to the Magistrate seeking reliefs under this Act. The Magistrate must fix the first hearing within 3 days and dispose of the application within 60 days.

title: Right to reside in shared household
text: Every woman in a domestic relationship has the right to reside in the shared household, whether or not she has any title or rights in the property.

title: Protection orders
text: The Magistrate may pass protection orders prohibiting the respondent from committing any act of domestic violence, aiding or abetting violence, entering the aggrieved person’s workplace, communicating with her, or dispossessing her from shared household.

title: Residence orders
text: The Magistrate may direct the respondent to remove himself from the shared household, restrain him from dispossessing the aggrieved person, or direct alternate accommodation.

title: Monetary reliefs
text: The Magistrate may direct payment of monetary relief to meet the expenses incurred due to domestic violence, loss of earnings, medical expenses, loss or damage to property, and maintenance.

title: Custody orders
text: The Magistrate may grant temporary custody of any child to the aggrieved person and specify visitation rights for the respondent, if any.

title: Compensation orders
text: The Magistrate may order the respondent to pay compensation for the injuries, including mental torture and emotional distress, caused by acts of domestic violence.

title: Service of notice
text: The Magistrate shall ensure that a notice of the application is served on the respondent and Protection Officer within a maximum of 2 days from the date of its receipt.

title: Power to grant interim and ex parte orders
text: The Magistrate may pass interim orders or ex parte orders based on the affidavit of the aggrieved person if immediate protection is required.

title: Duration and alteration of orders
text: Orders passed under this Act shall remain in force until revoked or modified by the Magistrate upon receiving application by either party.

title: Relief available under other laws
text: The aggrieved person may seek reliefs available under this Act in addition to any other legal proceedings she may have initiated under other laws.

title: Jurisdiction
text: The Magistrate’s court where the aggrieved person resides or where the incident of domestic violence occurred shall have jurisdiction to entertain applications under this Act.

title: Procedure
text: Proceedings under Sections 12, 18 to 23 and offences under Section 31 shall be governed by the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973, while the rest shall follow Civil Procedure as prescribed.

title: Appeal
text: Any person aggrieved by the order of the Magistrate may prefer an appeal to the Sessions Court within 30 days from the date of the order.

title: Penalty for breach of protection order
text: Breach of a protection order or interim protection order shall be an offence punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine up to twenty thousand rupees, or both.
